sentence_reviews.text: Information is represented by a small active subset of features, improving compression, interpretability, or conditional computation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML/DLbook sparse representation and regularization context; sparsely-gated MoE reference for conditional computation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Sparse coding supports small active feature subsets and can aid compression/interpretability; conditional computation is supported by sparse MoE examples. The sentence combines multiple sparse-representation uses.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Sparse coding & compositional representations is to connect Embeddings & distributed representations, Information theory to downstream capabilities including Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML/DLbook representation-learning and sparse coding context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Embeddings and sparse representations are supported. The information-theory and mechanistic-interpretability/circuit-analysis links are plausible but not directly supported by packet sources.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML supports sparse/learned representations generally, but not mechanistic interpretability and circuit analysis specifically.
sentence_reviews.notes: Sparse features are relevant to interpretability, but exact support for mechanistic circuit analysis needs a specific interpretability source.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML/DLbook discussions of distributed representations and sparse representations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Sparse codes can be treated as distributed representations with few active components; this is synthesized from representation-learning material.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Embeddings & distributed representations, Information theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML is relevant to representations but does not directly support the generic limitation sentence.
sentence_reviews.notes: The limitation text appears generic and is not directly supported for sparse coding by the candidate sources.

sentence_reviews.text: Sparse coding & compositional representations consumes, emits, or organizes information using Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML/DLbook representation-learning material covering sparse and distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Sparse codes are representation vectors, supporting this represents_with relation.

sentence_reviews.text: Sparse coding & compositional representations rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Information theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML includes information-theoretic framing for machine learning, but the packet source does not directly make this a sparse-coding dependency.
sentence_reviews.notes: Compression links sparse representations to information theory, but the source support is indirect and the dependency may be overstated as core.

sentence_reviews.text: Sparse coding & compositional representations rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Optimization theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML/DLbook discuss learning sparse representations through optimization objectives/regularization.
sentence_reviews.notes: Learning sparse codes commonly requires optimization, although hand-designed sparse representations need not.

edge_reviews.notes: Sparse codes are a form of vector/distributed representation with few active features; represents_with/core is defensible.
Compression provides an information-theoretic connection, but the packet evidence does not establish information theory as a core prerequisite.
Optimization is commonly used to learn sparse codes and sparse regularized representations, supporting a common dependency.
